'''Joe Cator''' (born 15 June 1998) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Championship and the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Hull KR]] at the [[Coventry Bears]] and the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]]. Cator also played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Cator was born in [[Kingston Upon Hull]], East Riding of Yorkshire, England.

==Playing career==
===Hull KR===
Cator began his career with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and was part of the side which was promoted back to the [[Super League]].<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20211209002440/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/joe-cator-hull-fc-leigh-3452058/ Joe Cator feels he's vindicated decision to leave Hull KR early after earning Hull FC move]</ref> He then spent time on loan at [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] before signing for his boyhood club [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/joe-cator-hull-fc-leigh-3452058/|title=Joe Cator feels he's vindicated decision to leave Hull KR early after earning Hull FC move|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
Cator played 11 games for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League XXV|2020 Super League]] season including the club's semi-final defeat against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. Cator had a loan spell with [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] during the early stages of 2020.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-hull-fc-highlights-4716172#964303/|title=Wigan Warriors v Hull FC highlights as FC struggle to find play-off form in one-sided defeat|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|date=19 November 2020 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-new-boy-joe-cator-says-dream-chance-has-been-long-time-coming-2440492/|title=Hull FC new boy Joe Cator says dream chance has been long time coming|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref> When he returned to Hull, he became one of the standout performers and went on to win the club's Young Player of the Year award at the end of the season. Cator played 26 matches for Hull F.C. in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=2023-10-05|archive-date=2023-10-04|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
On 26 October 2024, it was confirmed that he had agreed a deal with [[Toulouse Olympique]] ahead of the 2025 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=26 Oct 2024|title=Ex-Hull FC man Joe Cator secures deal for 2025|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-hull-fc-man-joe-cator-secures-deal-for-2025/|author=Callum Walker}}</ref>
